home.subtitle: Archive-first software recovery
home.title: Old software can live again.
home.description: Legacy Revival Studio restores aging web services, internal tools, and forgotten systems so their code, data, and behavior can run again on modern infrastructure.

core_principle.title: Core principle
core_principle.quote: Revive first. Rewrite only when necessary.
core_principle.preserve_desc: Source snapshots, database dumps, configuration, and file structure remain intact.
core_principle.runnable_desc: A revived system starts, performs core functions, and has its behavior documented.

philosophy.title: Preservation is an engineering practice.
philosophy.description: Legacy Revival Studio treats old systems as working artifacts: imperfect, specific, historically useful, and worth understanding before they are changed.

disappears.title: Software fades away
disappears.body: Without active maintenance, code becomes unrunnable. We reverse this decay.

data.title: State is historical context
data.body: A database is a record of human interaction. We preserve data as artifacts.

behavior.title: Behavior over perfection
behavior.body: We don't rewrite code to be modern. We modernize the environment to keep original behavior.

workflow.title: Human-guided. AI-assisted. Git-preserved.
workflow.description: The studio manages the entire restoration process systematically using modern workflows.
workflow.git_phil_1: Every revival is tracked in Git.

recover.title: Recover
recover.body: Collect source code, assets, and historical context securely without any alterations.

analyze.title: Analyze
analyze.body: Human-guided and AI-assisted precision analysis of legacy dependencies and system behavior.

restore.title: Restore
restore.body: Minimal and careful modernization that preserves original behavior instead of full rewrites.

archive.title: Archive
archive.body: Every meaningful change and decision is recorded in Git so nothing is silently lost.

relaunch.title: Relaunch
relaunch.body: Software runs stably again on modern infrastructure, ready for future maintenance.

services.title: What can be requested from the studio.
services.description: Legacy Revival Studio takes on specific restoration tasks for aging, abandoned, or undocumented systems.

review.title: Legacy Review
review.body: Analyze the structure and state of old codebases.

assessment.title: Revival Assessment
assessment.body: Assess feasibility of restoration, outline required tasks and identify potential risks.

modernization.title: Code Modernization
modernization.body: Modernize frameworks, dependencies, and infrastructure while preserving original behavior.

full_revival.title: Full Revival
full_revival.body: End-to-end restoration to bring an old service fully back to a runnable state.

rules: Preserve source code, database dumps, configuration, and file structure.
Separate raw artifacts from runtime, analysis, migration, and documentation work.
Document behavior before compatibility fixes or modernization patches.
Prefer small, reversible changes that reduce migration risk.

contact.title: Have a system everyone is afraid to touch?
contact.description: Start by making it run again. Then decide, with evidence, what should be preserved, repaired, or modernized.
contact.title_page: Start with the artifacts you still have.
contact.description_page: A source folder, database dump, old server backup, or partial documentation can be enough to begin a recovery conversation. No matter how old or tangled the code is, the original behavior and valuable data are strictly preserved, so please share with confidence.
contact.email_note: Including key data and context that must not be lost, such as the original runtime, database type, and last working date, in your first message allows for a faster and more accurate restoration simulation.

footer.slogan: Bring old software back to life.

project_preview.title: Restored systems should be visible, not forgotten.
project_preview.description: The archive will collect recovery notes, original context, runtime decisions, and modernization boundaries for each revived project.

project_grid.ready: The project archive is ready for future entries. Each record can link to a dedicated detail page with context, artifacts, runtime notes, and preservation decisions.

projects_page.title: A growing archive of revived software.
projects_page.description: Each project record is designed to preserve context: what was found, how it was made runnable, which behaviors were documented, and what changed.

philosophy_page.title: Bring old software back to life, functionally.
philosophy_page.description: Legacy Revival Studio is not about redesigning history. It is about preserving digital artifacts well enough that they can run, be studied, and remain useful.

about_page.title: A small studio for systems that still matter.
about_page.description: Legacy Revival Studio grows from the habit of revisiting old projects, making them run again, and refusing to let useful digital work disappear quietly.
about_page.p1: The studio focuses on aging web services, internal tools, undocumented databases, and codebases that organizations still depend on but hesitate to touch.
about_page.p2: The work is deliberately modest: recover artifacts, recreate a runtime, document behavior, and only then decide which parts should be modernized.
about_page.p3: Over time, the public site will also serve as an archive of revived projects and the technical decisions that helped preserve them.

not_found.title: This archive record does not exist yet.
not_found.description: The site is structured for future project detail pages, but this route does not currently match a published record.

nightdance.title: NightDance Homepage
nightdance.summary: Restored an early 2000s PHP/MySQL community board. Successfully containerized and isolated legacy dependencies while maintaining full original functionality.
notes: Legacy mysql_* functions were safely wrapped using a PDO proxy.
Directory structures were preserved exactly as they were in 2001.

holapet.title: Hola Pet Abandoned Animals Platform
holapet.summary: Migrated the abandoned animal adoption service of Hola Pet to a cloud-based delivery environment integrated with GitHub Actions to bring it back to a fully runnable state.
holapet.figCaption: [Fig 2. Hola Pet Abandoned Animals Platform Revived Mobile Screenshot, 2025]
notes: Cleaned up redundant fields and tables from the legacy database, and successfully migrated shelter information and adoption posts data.
Completely revamped the UI framework from obsolete Bootstrap 3 to modern Tailwind CSS 3 for an updated, streamlined user interface.
Upgraded core runtimes from PHP 7.3 to 8.3, Laravel 8 to 12, and MySQL 5.7 to 8.0 while writing complete functional tests to guarantee code integrity.
Implemented automated Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipelines using GitHub Actions, eliminating the need for any manual deployments.

template.title: Archive Record Template
template.summary: A lightweight example record showing how future restoration projects can preserve context, runtime notes, and modernization boundaries.
template.figCaption: [Fig 3. Archive Record Directory Schema Template Snapshot, 2024]
notes: Future project records should begin with what was found: source snapshots, database dumps, configuration, screenshots, server notes, and any surviving operational context.
Each record should separate recovery work from modernization work so readers can understand which behavior is original and which change was introduced for compatibility.
When a real restoration is published, this template can be replaced by the first project archive entry.
